chartPattern.explanation: The price action shows a rounded bottom forming a cup from late January to mid-March 2026, with lows around $85. The subsequent consolidation from mid-March to early May forms a handle, with prices drifting down to around $148 before rallying. The recent price action on 2026-06-01 and 2026-06-02 shows a potential breakout from this handle.
chartPattern.signal: A confirmed breakout above the handle's resistance, ideally on increased volume, could signal a continuation of the prior uptrend, with potential upside targets based on the depth of the cup.

fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong analyst estimates are offset by current unprofitability and high valuation multiples.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — very high expected growth.
Forward P/E 42.06x with PEG 0.78x — suggests growth may outpace valuation.
Current ratio 3.83x and Quick ratio 2.85x — strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — currently unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — negative returns.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Last quarter EPS -0.07 vs estimate -0.05 (surprise -44.8%) — earnings miss.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention following a "risk-on" guidance, with analysts expressing bullish sentiment and highlighting its strong performance relative to peers. The stock has climbed substantially year-to-date, driven by a large upgrade deal with Mediacom and its position within the chip sector. Despite a recent earnings miss, the forward outlook and analyst estimates suggest strong growth ahead.
